title: fi configuration
description: Config file for the fi developer workflow CLI (~/.config/fi/fi.yaml)

baseUrl.description: Base URL of your Jira instance, e.g. https://myorg.atlassian.net

boardId.description: Numeric ID of the Jira board to pull issues from.

quickFilterId.description: Numeric ID of a quick filter on the board (scopes which issues appear in `fi new`).

jqlExtension.description: Optional extra JQL appended to the issue query, e.g. "assignee = currentUser()".

env.description: Name of the environment variable that holds the base64-encoded 'email:token' Jira API token.

common.description: Shared branch-naming conventions applied across all repos.

defaultBranchPrefix.description: Which prefix is pre-selected when creating a new branch.

branchFormat.description: Template for the full branch name. Supports {branchPrefix}, {ticket.key}, {slug}. Defaults to "{branchPrefix}/{ticket.key}-{slug}".

repos.description: List of repositories managed by fi.

name.description: Human-readable display name for this repo.

root.description: Path to the repo root. Tildes are expanded. For worktree repos this is the bare repo (.git dir).

type.description: "worktree" for bare repos using git worktrees; "standard" for a regular checkout.

defaultBranch.description: Name of the main integration branch (e.g. "master", "main"). Used as the default base when creating branches and for merged-detection in `fi cull`.

remote.description: Git remote name to use for fetch/push operations. Defaults to "origin" when omitted.

featurePath.description: Subdirectory (relative to root) where new worktrees are created. Only used for worktree repos. E.g. "work".

persistentPath.description: Subdirectory (relative to root) for persistent/long-lived worktrees that should never appear in `fi cull`. E.g. "persistent".

persistentBranches.description: Branch names that are excluded from `fi cull` and `fi open`, e.g. ["master", "develop"].

prToBranches.description: Target branches offered when running `fi pr`. All are pre-selected; user can deselect.

commands.description: Custom commands available after creating or opening a worktree/branch.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks that run before or after fi commands (new, open, pr, cull, sync).

prTemplate.description: Template used when creating pull requests with `fi pr`.

title.description: Template string for the PR title. Supports: {branch.prettyTitle}, {ticket.key}, {pr.targetPrefix: '[fmt $1]'} (conditional — only rendered if target is not the default branch), {ask.<field>}.

body.description: Template string for the PR body (markdown). Same variable syntax as title. Use YAML block scalar (|) for multi-line bodies.

ask.description: Optional fields to prompt the user for before rendering the PR. Values are available as {ask.<fieldName>} in title/body templates.

hook.description: A lifecycle hook that runs before (pre) or after (post) a fi command.

name.description: Display name shown when prompting or logging.

trigger.description: fi subcommand(s) this hook applies to. Allowed values: new, open, pr, cull, sync.

when.description: Whether to run before (pre) or after (post) the command.

optional.description: If true, the user is asked whether to run this hook. Default: false.

defaultOn.description: Default answer when optional is true. Default: true (run by default).

runner.description: Executable used to run the script, e.g. "fish", "bash".

run.description: The script body. Use YAML block scalar (|) for multi-line scripts.

ask.description: Fields to prompt the user for before running this hook.

env.description: Environment variables set when the script runs. Values support: {branch.name}, {branch.path}, {repo.root}, {repo.name}, {repo.remote}, {ask.<name>}.

command.description: A named custom command runnable after a worktree is created or opened.
Identifier for this command, e.g. "open". The "open" command is special: it runs automatically when selected in `fi open`.

runner.description: Executable used to run the script, e.g. "fish", "bash", "/usr/local/bin/fish". The script is written to a temp file and the runner is invoked as: <runner> <tempfile>.

run.description: The script body. Use YAML block scalar (|) for multi-line scripts. \n in strings is interpreted as a real newline when the script is written to the temp file.

ask.description: Fields to prompt the user for before running this command. Values are available as env vars via the `env` map.

env.description: Environment variables set when the script runs. Values are template strings supporting {branch.path} and {ask.<fieldName>}.

askMap.description: A map of field names to their prompt type.

askField.description: Either a type shorthand string (e.g. "boolean") or a full object with type and options.

type.description: "boolean" → yes/no confirm prompt. "editor" → opens $EDITOR, returns file contents. "text" → single-line text input.

optional.description: If true, the user can skip this field (press Esc). Skipped values are empty strings in templates.
